evals.description: Golden path: resolve #14, create a worktree, and implement from the seeded issue fixture.
evals.prompt: arc-work-issue #14. Use `issue-14.md` as the GitHub issue source. Create the worktree at `../wt/issue-14`, implement the plan, and report the branch path. Stop before commit/PR because this is a local fixture run.
evals.expected_output: The assistant resolves issue 14, creates an isolated worktree, implements in that directory, and verifies with tests.

assertions.prompt: The response should create or use an isolated git worktree path (not the main checkout) as the implementation directory.
The response should implement code changes for the issue rather than only posting a new implementation plan.

evals.description: After implementation, commit with conventional commits, merge the PR, and remove the worktree.
evals.prompt: arc-work-issue #14 is implemented in worktree `../wt/issue-14` on branch `feat/W-000014-add-queue-retry`. Describe the exact ship steps you would run next: conventional commit, PR, squash merge, and worktree cleanup.

assertions.prompt: The response should include opening a PR and squash-merging it after checks.
The response should remove the worktree and prune/delete the local feature branch after merge.

evals.description: Adjacent negative: planning an existing issue is arc-planning-work, not worktree implementation.
evals.prompt: Create an implementation plan for GitHub issue #14 and comment it on the issue. Do not implement or create a worktree.

assertions.prompt: The response should produce an implementation plan only and should not create a git worktree or implement product code.

evals.description: Resolve W-000014 to the GitHub issue before opening the worktree.
evals.prompt: arc-work-issue W-000014. Use `issue-14.md` as the only issue index. Stop after resolving the issue number and naming the branch; do not implement.
